operating_state: Second quarter 2025 earnings of $7.1 billion decreased from $9.2 billion driven by weaker crude oil prices and lower chemical realizations, partially offset by increased volumes from advantaged Upstream investments in the Permian and Guyana. Upstream production reached 4.6 million oil-equivalent barrels per day, increasing primarily due to the Pioneer acquisition. Energy Products earnings increased driven by improved industry refining margins and lower scheduled maintenance.
cost_structure: Structural cost savings contributed $350 million with cumulative savings of $13.5 billion relative to 2019 levels. Chemical Products margins weakened significantly due to lower North America ethane feed advantage and continued industry oversupply. Operating expenses increased from growth initiatives and higher depreciation, while Corporate and Financing expenses rose due to lower interest income and unfavorable foreign exchange impacts.
material_events: The Corporation distributed dividends and repurchased $9.8 billion of common stock during the first six months of 2025. Karen T. McKee, president of ExxonMobil Product Solutions Company, announced her retirement effective May 1, 2025. The annual shareholder meeting was held on May 28, 2025, with all twelve director nominees elected and ratification of independent auditors and executive compensation approved.
macro_exposures: The U.S. announced various trade-related actions including tariffs on imports from several countries, with many countries announcing retaliatory tariffs in response. Certain tariffs were paused but not withdrawn while others have been revised, creating a volatile global trade environment. The likelihood of resuming tariffs, imposing new reciprocal tariffs, export restrictions, or other trade-related sanctions remains highly uncertain, though the Corporation does not anticipate material near-term financial impacts.
forward_outlook: The Corporation plans to invest in the range of $27 billion to $29 billion in 2025, with actual spending varying based on project progress and property acquisitions. Capital allocation priorities remain investing in competitively advantaged high-return projects, maintaining a strong balance sheet, and sharing success with shareholders through consistent share repurchases and growing dividends.
uncertainty_profile: Actions needed to advance 2030 greenhouse gas emission-reduction plans are incorporated into medium-term business plans updated annually, with future policies and technology advancements to be incorporated as they emerge. Individual projects may advance based on stable policy support, permitting, technological advancement for cost-effective abatement, and alignment with partners.
